ogTitle: Potts Hill vs Pottsville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Potts Hill and Pottsville.
heading: Potts Hill vs Pottsville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Potts Hill, New South Wales and Pottsville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pottsville has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Potts Hill (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Potts Hill or Pottsville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Potts Hill has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Pottsville scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Potts Hill scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Pottsville scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Potts Hill or Pottsville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pottsville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
